title: helianthemum apenninum subsp. suffruticosum
intro: Helianthemum apenninum subsp. suffruticosum, also known as alpine rockrose, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Cistaceae, characterized by its pink to purple flowers and shrubby growth habit. Traditionally, the plant has been used in folk medicine for its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which are attributed to its content of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, soothing digestive issues, and exhibiting antioxidant properties, although more research is needed to confirm these effects. In herbal preparations, alpine rockrose is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures to leverage its medicinal properties, but it's essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using it for therapeutic purposes.
